content: Hey Saturday, I just started piano lessons with Professor Kim Jae-won last week. I’m basically a total beginner.

That’s awesome—starting with a great teacher makes a huge difference. If you stay consistent early on, your progress will feel slow at first but very solid. What are you working on in lessons right now?
Right now it’s mostly Hanon exercises and a couple of really simple Bach minuets. I’m trying not to rush.

That’s a perfect beginner combo: Hanon for finger control, Bach for clarity and phrasing. Going slowly with good rhythm and relaxed hands is exactly the right mindset. Professor Kim Jae-won sounds like he’s setting you up well.
I’m doing 30 minutes a day: 10 min scales, 10 min sight-reading, 10 min repertoire. Do you think that’s enough?

Yes, 30 focused minutes daily is enough to build real momentum as a beginner. Your split is excellent and balanced, so keep it steady rather than cramming longer sessions randomly. If possible, track tempo weekly so you can see small wins.
My big goal is to play a Chopin Nocturne within 18 months. Is that realistic from zero?

Yes—within 18 months is realistic with your consistency and Professor Kim Jae-won guiding technique. You’ll likely need intermediate steps first (more Bach, easy Romantic pieces, pedal control, rubato basics). Keep your routine, and you can absolutely reach a simpler Chopin Nocturne by then.
